questions.question: Which brain structure acts as the body's master biological clock, regulating the circadian rhythm based on light exposure?
questions.explanation: The suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN), located in the hypothalamus, receives light information from the eyes and uses it to synchronize the body's circadian rhythm with the external light-dark cycle. It signals the pineal gland to release melatonin when light decreases.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes consciousness?

options.text: A continuum of awareness ranging from full alertness to deep unconsciousness
A process controlled entirely by the endocrine system
A fixed state that is either fully on or fully off
A state that only occurs during wakefulness and disappears entirely during sleep

questions.explanation: Consciousness exists on a continuum rather than being an all-or-nothing state. It ranges from full, focused awareness through daydreaming and automatic processing to sleep and deep unconsciousness, with varying levels of awareness at each point.
